findings.evidence: `nl -ba` shows `map : ... →ₐc[K] ...` at lines 157-159 and only `counit_map`/`map_comp_comul_apply` at lines 229-244; `rg -n "HopfHom|→ₐh|antipode.*(comp|map|apply)|map.*antipode|BialgHom.*antipode" Mathlib/RingTheory/HopfAlgebra Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra` finds no existing antipode-preservation API, while `Mathlib/RingTheory/HopfAlgebra/Basic.lean` lists uniqueness of antipodes as a TODO.
findings.fix: Add the Hopf-level characteristic lemma under `[HopfAlgebra k A] [HopfAlgebra k B]`, e.g. `map_antipode`/`antipode_map`, proving `map f (HopfAlgebra.antipode k x)` commutes with the scalar-extended antipode, or expose an appropriate Hopf morphism abstraction if one is added.
findings.issue: `BaseChange.map` is the public scalar-extension map for Hopf base change, but it only returns a `BialgHom` and the API stops at counit/comultiplication, so users cannot use it with the antipode operation without unfolding or reproving compatibility.

summary: The base-change object and inclusion API are present, but the public functorial API does not characterize scalar extension as Hopf-algebra functoriality because it exposes only bialgebra-map compatibility and no antipode compatibility.
